Package org.eventb.core

Interface IPOStampedElement

All Known Subinterfaces:
IPOPredicateSet, IPORoot, IPOSequent, IPSStatus
All Known Implementing Classes:
ContextRoot, EventBProofElement, EventBRoot, MachineRoot, POPredicateSet, PORoot, POSequent, PRHypAction, PRProof, PRProofRule, PRRoot, PRRuleAntecedent, PSRoot, PSStatus, SCContextRoot, SCMachineRoot
public interface IPOStampedElement
Proof obligation stamps signal changes in PO sequents (i.e. proof obligations) and predicate sets (i.e. hypotheses). One stamp is associated with a PO file. If any sequent or predicate set has changed, the PO file stamp is changed too. Deletion and insertion do both increase the PO file stamp.

The PO file stamp is related to the maximal stamp used in the PO file: If some sequent or predicate sets changes, the PO file stamp is incremented. This same stamp is then used for all changed sequents and predicate sets. Any change in a PO file increases the PO file stamp, even if none of the remaining proof obligations (or predicate sets) have changed, e.g., after a deletion. This means, the PO file stamp my be larger than any other stamp used in the PO file.

Since:
1.0
See Also:
  • IPORoot
  • IPOSequent
  • IPOPredicateSet
  • IPSRoot
  • IPSStatus

    getPOStamp()
    Returns the stamp of this element.

    Tells whether this element is stamped.

    Sets the stamp of this element.

  • Field Details

    • INIT_STAMP

      static final long INIT_STAMP
      The initial value to be used for stamps. That is, initially the stamp of an element should be set to INIT_STAMP: 
       setStamp(INIT_STAMP, monitor);
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Method Details

    • setPOStamp

      void setPOStamp(long stamp, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor monitor) throws RodinDBException
      Sets the stamp of this element.
      Parameters:
      stamp - the stamp to set
      monitor - a progress monitor, or null if progress reporting is not desired
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • hasPOStamp

      boolean hasPOStamp() throws RodinDBException
      Tells whether this element is stamped.
      Returns:
      true iff this element has a stamp
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database

    • getPOStamp

      long getPOStamp() throws RodinDBException
      Returns the stamp of this element.
      Returns:
      the stamp of this element
      Throws:
      RodinDBException - if there was a problem accessing the database
